articles.title: Advantages and Limitations of AlphaFold in Structural Biology: Insights from Recent Studies
articles.summary: this narrative review synthesizes applications reported in the 2022–2025 literature. representative examples include modeling of SARS-CoV-2 spike and nucleocapsid proteins in virology, assisting cryo-EM interpretation of bacterial ribosomal and membrane-protein complexes in microbiology, and refining conformational hypotheses for human GPCRs.

articles.title: MAKA-Map: Real-Valued Distance Prediction for Protein Folding Mechanisms via a Hybrid Neural Framework Integrating the Mamba and Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks
articles.summary: real-valued distance maps provide essential spatial information for understanding protein folding mechanisms and guiding downstream applications such as function annotation, drug discovery, and structural modeling. the proposed framework integrates a selective state space model to effectively model global interactions and incorporates the Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (KAN)

articles.title: Efficient global accuracy estimation for protein complex structural models using multi-view representation learning
articles.summary: MViewEMA is a single-model EMA method that leverages multi-view representation learning framework to integrate residue-residue interaction features from micro-environment, meso, and macro. the method achieved top performance in model selection tracks during the CASP16 blind test, demonstrating its potential to enhance the accuracy of complex structure prediction when integrated into modern frameworks such as AlphaFold-Multimer and DiffDock-PP.

articles.title: Recent advances in machine learning and coarse-grained potentials for biomolecular simulations
articles.summary: biomolecular simulations face inherent challenges due to multiscale nature of biological processes. applications ranging from drug discovery to the molecular characterization of virus-host interactions are a key challenge in the study of the complex dynamics in biological systems.

articles.title: Connecting the dots: deep learning-based automated model building methods in cryo-EM
articles.summary: cryo-EM density maps enable atomic model building, but lower-resolution maps can still reveal secondary structures, folds, and domains. deep learning offers powerful new tools to automate and accelerate this process, assessing their impact on streamlining structure determination.
